author: @thejoe500iq
text: Hahah did not think I would ever be looking where to buy bamboo. I'm a scaffolder by trade and the guys that use the stuff for absolutely massive scaffolding really fights my doubt for its strength. How can I argue with that? Great stuff as always! Awesome project!

replies.author: @ivanolsen8596
replies.text: Was in a typhoon in Hong Kong some years back, what has stayed in my mind was the TV vision of buildings with steel scaffolding that had collapsed while the bamboo  scaffolding stayed in place.

author: @patbutcher1754
text: Like this a lot! A suggestion if I may - if you still have it, why not try your EESD/battery pack from your  electric scooter, instead of those heavy lead acid batteries. It would be interesting to see what performance you could get from that combination, and the weight reduction would be considerable. This is assuming that the voltages are the same or similar, of course.

replies.author: @colleenforrest7936
replies.text:
@richardlilley6274 Haha :)  
It depends on the species.  Even Moso can stand some dry and cold. Enough to keep the chocolate happy anyway. Besides, this stuff would have to be grown under the domes with the people's.  You get enough people in a confined space and the air is going to get warm and wet. So you'd have to figure out the right ratio of humans to bamboo plants to create the optimum symbiosis :)

author: @johnsshed995
text: Great project ,nicely done .But I would question the legality of useing on UK roads or even cycle paths ,look out for blue flashing lights behind you.I look forward to the next one ;-)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it'll be fine if I stick some pedals on mate and I am thinking of doing exactly that

replies.author: @bugz000
replies.text:
​@ThinkingandTinkering low powered electric vehicles must be road registered, only "assisted pedal" bikes (and i think restricted to two wheels) are permitted to be used with no licence, with only power applied while pedalling, below a certain torque of "assist" and only assisting up to a max of 15mph, AND there must be absolutley no method to power the vehicle without pedalling, that is, a throttle control is not permitted 

the laws in the uk are absolutely ridiculous, we are pretty much the last country on earth to actively ban e-scooters/similar on cycle-lanes... it's absurd, only a few cities in the UK are running "government approved" e-scooters within a set mile radius of the city center and only to lower emissions...

thankfully Halfords is in on the fight against these stupid laws, showing an online map that beautifully displays how isolated we are on our stance against electric vehicles, and openly sell e-scooters in all their stores despite there being no legal way to run them on roads (As they are too slow to be road registered in themselves, they are assumed to be for private land ONLY)

it's been at least 3 years since the "12 month escooter trials" and there's been no change to the laws yet... frustrating doesn't begin to describe it

author: @Nuts-Bolts
text: Thinks. If it weighs no more than 60 kilograms (130lb), including the battery; and its motor not rated at any more than 250 watts, then it will be free of any requirement for excise duty nor driving licence. Therefore, it could be driven by a 14 year old.

replies.author: @CraigDavies.
replies.text: Don't forget it the throttle / speed controller must be activated by the pedals. Over on the pedelecs forum you can find a thread explaining all the relative laws. Did you know electric qaudricycles are not allowed on bridleways only bikes and 3 wheelers. Technically no home built electric vehicle is road legal . Don't you just love are government.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: interesting - cheers

replies.author: @CASHSEC
replies.text: That's my question answered but what I must do now is to research the law further to find out the complete matrix as I would want a pedaless  vehicle that would do about 30 mph and provide some weather cover.

replies.author: @CraigDavies.
replies.text: @CASHSEC You need the MSVA manual available on line. It contains every thing you need to know about passing the MSVA. To enable you to register your home built vehicle, what you propose would come under light qaudricycle.
